h_os_conv.name: h_os_conv
h_os_conv.meaning: The vacuum states of H(K,N) converge in PhysH-norm to the physical vacuum as K,N->inf
requires: h_rp: reflection positivity of phi^4_2 Hamiltonian truncation (open, not in Lean4/Mathlib)
Glimm-Jaffe-Spencer ground-state convergence (proved in physics literature 1974, not yet in Lean4)

theorem_content.abstract: If a sequence of vacuum states vac_K converges in a Hilbert space PhysH, and phi_0 and time_ev are bounded linear operators on PhysH, then the Euclidean two-point Schwinger function <(phi_0 o time_ev o phi_0)(vac_K), vac_K> converges.
theorem_content.physical_meaning: The OS two-point Schwinger function S_K(t) = <Omega_K, phi(0,t)phi(0,0) Omega_K> converges as K,N->inf, establishing the existence of the limiting Schwinger function S(t) from which Wightman reconstruction applies.
theorem_content.reduction: Reduces to inner_bilinear_convergence with operator A = phi_0 o time_ev o phi_0 (composition of bounded linear operators).
